name: Snap-on Inc
business: Snap-on is a leading global innovator, manufacturer and marketer of tools, equipment, diagnostics, repair information and systems solutions for professional users performing critical tasks including vehicle repair, aerospace, military, natural resources, and manufacturing. Products are sold through franchisee vans, direct and distributor channels in more than 130 countries, with the largest market being the United States.
sellsTo: commercial and industrial customers in critical industries

exposures.sentence: While new technologies, including those associated with alternative energy drivetrains and greater vehicle autonomy, may alter the nature of certain service and repair for particular vehicle types, we believe many of these new technologies provide opportunities to fulfill requirements for enhanced solutions or greater precision.
The development of emerging vehicle drivetrains and advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) has increased in recent years, and further innovation is expected to continue in the future.
Further building our proprietary databases to enhance software solutions, including using artificial intelligence ( AI ) to accelerate expansion in that arena.
technological developments and enhancements of products and service offerings in our industry may require our expanded use of artificial intelligence ( AI )

Snap-on markets its products and services globally to a broad cross-section of commercial and industrial customers, including ... various government agencies, facilities and operations, including military operations;
Snap-on markets its products and services globally to a broad cross-section of commercial and industrial customers, including ... aviation and aerospace operations;
Snap-on markets its products and services globally to a broad cross-section of commercial and industrial customers, including ... oil and gas developers;
Snap-on markets its products and services globally to a broad cross-section of commercial and industrial customers, including ... mining operations;
